title: Dactylorhiza × ruppertii
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × ruppertii is a hybrid species within the genus Dactylorhiza, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a cross between two different species of Dactylorhiza, resulting in a unique combination of characteristics. The classification of Dactylorhiza × ruppertii is therefore as a hybrid species, with its parents being Dactylorhiza maculata and Dactylorhiza fuchsii. The classification is based on the study of its morphology, anatomy, and genetic characteristics. This hybrid species is considered an important part of the Dactylorhiza genus.
uses: Dactylorhiza × ruppertii has been reported to have potential medicinal uses in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential benefits. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could contribute to its medicinal value. Traditional healers have used Dactylorhiza × ruppertii to treat conditions such as respiratory issues and digestive problems. Further studies are required to confirm its medicinal uses and potential applications.

constituents: Dactylorhiza × ruppertii contains flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant also contains iridoids and other compounds that have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Iridoids like 7-O-methylhastatoside and 7-O- methylasperuloside have been identified in the plant, showcasing its unique chemical profile. The presence of these active constituents supports the use of Dactylorhiza × ruppertii in traditional medicine, particularly for its potential benefits on cardiovascular health and inflammation. Further research is required to fully understand the effects of these constituents on human health.
